I do not fully agree with the initial claims linking TPP directly to glycogen phosphorylase. Although TPP plays an important role in glycolysis—specifically in the conversion of pyruvate to acetyl-CoA through pyruvate dehydrogenase—it does not serve as a cofactor for glycogen phosphorylase itself. Instead, glycogen phosphorylase operates independently using no known vitamin-derived coenzyme such as TPP, PLP, riboflavin, or lipoic acid.
